prompt:
Our customer model stopped persisting under the framework 7.2 encrypted-attribute runtime. Startup validation reports `Customer.contact_code uses removed encrypts encrypted-attribute API`. The upgrade also coincides with the scheduled key rotation, so a startup-only workaround is not enough: archived rows and the prior rotation batch still have to load, while all new rows must use the current write scheme. Protected local framework notes and two fake rotation ciphertexts capture that boundary offline.

Please migrate the model integration and rotation configuration. Preserve plaintext validation and its field errors, ensure rejected records neither write nor consume encryption state, keep reads side-effect free, and make repeated new writes of the same value produce distinct authenticated ciphertext under the primary scheme. Unknown schemes and tampering must remain explicit decryption failures rather than blank values. Do not rewrite old rows on read or modify the protected runtime and fixtures.

Run `ruby test_encryption_move.rb`. The test, framework surface, notes, and rotation fixtures are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: migrate a Rails-like encrypted model declaration and key-provider boundary while preserving validation timing and multi-generation ciphertext compatibility
full_distill.baseline_evidence: ruby test_encryption_move.rb exits nonzero because protected framework validation deterministically rejects Customer.contact_code's removed encrypts declaration
full_distill.success_contract: legacy and prior-rotation fixtures decrypt without writes, new saves use the nonce-bearing current scheme, plaintext validation runs before encryption, and authentication or unknown-scheme failures remain explicit
adversarial_factors: two protected fixtures use different versions and key identifiers, so retaining only the oldest ciphertext reader or only the immediately prior key does not satisfy rotation compatibility
the same plaintext is saved with two injected nonces and must yield distinct current-scheme ciphertexts, rejecting continued deterministic legacy writes or a hard-coded fixture answer
invalid records assert errors, zero writes, and zero nonce calls, preventing encrypt-then-validate ordering or exception swallowing
fixture reads pin byte-identical stored ciphertext and zero writes while tampered and unknown-key rows must raise DecryptionError, rejecting opportunistic read rotation and blank-value fallbacks
